metadata.title: Why Silencing a Repair Gene Is Better for the Patient Than Expressing It
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL=#1F6F5C = MGMT methylated / repair silenced / drug works / good patient outcome; CRIMSON=#BF3339 = MGMT expressed / damage repaired / drug neutralized / bad patient outcome
metadata.note: Exclusions honored: no full DNA methylation mechanism (DNMT1, maintenance methylation), no histone code, no IDH mutation connection, no azacitidine/decitabine mechanism, no other epigenetic biomarkers. One mechanism only: MGMT promoter methylation silences repair, allowing temozolomide damage to accumulate and kill tumor cells.
metadata.clock: narration (ElevenLabs) — durations below are word-count estimates until GATE 0 audio lock
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Wonder register (voices/wonder/VOICE.md + MEDHAVY.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the MEDHAVY outro from AUTHOR.MD :: Medhavy.com
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.name: Title
beats.narration_text: Two patients. Same glioblastoma, same surgery, same radiation, same chemotherapy. One responds. The other doesn't. The difference is a single methyl group.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Two patients. Same glioblastoma, same surgery, same radiation, same chemotherapy. One responds. The other doesn't. The difference is a single methyl group.

beats.name: TwoPatients
beats.narration_text: Glioblastoma is the most aggressive brain tumor we know. Most patients die within 15 months. And yet some respond to temozolomide far better than others — same drug, dramatically different outcomes.

props.headline: Glioblastoma — the most aggressive brain tumor — kills most patients…

beats.scene_description: Two medical charts side by side, pinned to newsprint — same diagnosis header, same treatment protocol listed — patient A and patient B, editorial collage style, desaturated

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Glioblastoma is the most aggressive brain tumor we know. Most patients die within 15 months. And yet some respond to temozolomide far better than others — same

beats.name: SameTreatment
beats.narration_text: Here is what the drug is doing. Temozolomide attaches a methyl group to the O6 position of guanine in tumor DNA. That lesion jams replication. The cell cannot copy its genome and it dies.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Here is what the drug is doing. Temozolomide attaches a methyl group to the O6 position of guanine in tumor DNA. That lesion jams replication. The cell cannot c

beats.name: TheQuestion
beats.narration_text: Here's where it gets strange. DNA repair should protect a cell. A tumor that fixes its own DNA more efficiently should survive better — and yet the patient whose tumor repairs well does worse. Why does better repair mean a worse outcome?

beats.name: NaiveExpectation
beats.narration_text: The naive expectation: more DNA repair should be better. We're damaging tumor DNA — we want that damage to kill the cancer. A cell that fixes its own damage should die less easily.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The naive expectation: more DNA repair should be better. We're damaging tumor DNA — we want that damage to kill the cancer. A cell that fixes its own damage sho

beats.name: WhyItFlips
beats.narration_text: The interesting part is the inversion. The drug is trying to damage the tumor. MGMT exists to reverse exactly that damage — and it does so in minutes. When MGMT is active, it neutralizes the drug before it can act.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The interesting part is the inversion. The drug is trying to damage the tumor. MGMT exists to reverse exactly that damage — and it does so in minutes. When MGMT

beats.name: DecisionTree
beats.narration_text: MGMT status splits patients into two paths. Methylated promoter: no MGMT protein made, O6-methylguanine lesions accumulate, tumor cells die. Unmethylated: MGMT expressed, damage repaired, drug wasted.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: MGMT status splits patients into two paths. Methylated promoter: no MGMT protein made, O6-methylguanine lesions accumulate, tumor cells die. Unmethylated: MGMT

beats.name: SuicideMechanism
beats.narration_text: Here's what's actually happening at the protein level. MGMT is a suicide enzyme. It transfers the methyl group from O6-guanine onto its own cysteine — one repair event per molecule. Then it destroys itself. It cannot be recycled.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Here's what's actually happening at the protein level. MGMT is a suicide enzyme. It transfers the methyl group from O6-guanine onto its own cysteine — one repai

beats.name: MethylSilences
beats.narration_text: In the methylated patient, cytosines in the MGMT promoter carry methyl marks. Transcription machinery cannot access the gene. No MGMT mRNA. No protein. The repair capacity is permanently switched off.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: In the methylated patient, cytosines in the MGMT promoter carry methyl marks. Transcription machinery cannot access the gene. No MGMT mRNA. No protein. The repa

beats.name: Biomarker
beats.narration_text: MGMT methylation is now standard in glioblastoma management — a predictive biomarker. Notice the distinction: it is not prognostic. It does not predict survival regardless of treatment. It predicts benefit from this specific drug.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: MGMT methylation is now standard in glioblastoma management — a predictive biomarker. Notice the distinction: it is not prognostic. It does not predict survival

beats.name: ExampleTrace
beats.narration_text: Follow one lesion in each patient — illustrative. Patient A: O6-methylguanine forms, MGMT is absent, the lesion persists through replication, mismatch-repair triggers apoptosis, tumor cell dies. Patient B: the same lesion forms, MGMT transfers the methyl group in roughly 2 minutes — cell survives, drug wasted.

beats.name: Endcard
beats.narration_text: DNA repair protects the cell — but when the drug is trying to destroy that cell, repair protects the wrong side. An epigenetic mark that silences the repair gene becomes, for the patient, a stroke of luck. The biology that looks like damage is actually the opening.
